title: flat_map with vec![] allocates a heap Vec per form
description: lib.rs:85-92: The extension validation uses flat_map returning vec![s] or v.iter()...collect() — allocating a heap Vec for every Form in the require statement. The idiomatic pattern is flat_map with std::iter::once or chain to produce an iterator without allocation. Better yet, skip the collect entirely and use nested loops with early return, which is clearer and zero-allocation.
